items.title: India shot down six Pakistani aircraft in May, air force chief says | REUTERS
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>India shot down five Pakistani fighter jets and one other military aircraft during clashes in May, India's air force chief said in the first such public claim by the country after its worst military conflict in decades with its neighbor. 

#india #pakistan #conflict #News #Reuters #Newsfeed

items.title: Rising temperatures threaten Croatia's largest coral reef | REUTERS
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>For over three decades, marine biologist Petar Kruzic has been descending into the depths of Croatia's Great Lake. But with the rise in temperature, Kruzic explains the challenges faced by the largest known Mediterranean sod coral reef.

#coralreef #croatia #MljetNationalPark #environment #globalwarming #News #Reuters #Newsfeed

items.title: Dancer's journey embodies the spirit of Benin's Mask Festival
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>As the vibrant rhythms of traditional drums fill the air, Oscar Ue-bi's eyes gleam behind his intricately carved Zaouli mask. The Ivorian performer twirls and leaps, his colorful outfit a blur of motion at Benin's second annual Mask Festival in Porto Novo.
 
#maskfestival #benin #dancer #tradition #Zaouli#News #Reuters #Newsfeed

items.title: Trump-Putin, Germany's Israel curb, $5 meals and Musk vs Modi
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article> U.S. President Donald Trump is set to meet Russian President Vladimir Putin in Alaska on August 15 to discuss a Ukraine ceasefire. Germany has suspended arms exports to Israel that could be used in the Gaza strip. Elon Musk’s X battles the Indian government on internet censorship. Plus, the fast food giants trying to reach low income Americans with $5 meals.








Listen to On Assignment (https://www.reuters.com/podcasts/on-assignment/)  podcast here.

items.title: Meet the German cuddling with his pet alligator
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>To cuddle with his pet requires an unusual amount of trust for Krystian Kaulis. “Frau Meyer” as he calls her (German for Mrs. Meyer), is an eight-foot, two-inch-long American Alligator. While she spends most of her time floating and sunbathing, she is perfectly able to move at lightning speed or crush food with her huge jaws and 82 teeth.
 
#alligator #pet #animal #frau #german</article>
